questions.question: Define a one-dimensional array in C and explain why arrays are used in programming.
keyPoints: Array is a collection of elements of same data type stored in contiguous memory
Single variable name with multiple elements accessed by index
Used to store multiple related values efficiently
Enables processing of collections like marks, temperatures, inventory

questions.question: Write a C program to find the sum and average of n elements stored in a one-dimensional array.
keyPoints: Initialize sum = 0
Use loop to traverse array and add each element
Calculate average as sum/n (use float for precision)
Handle edge case of n=0

questions.question: Explain the difference between int arr[5] and int arr[] with examples.
keyPoints: int arr[5] - allocates fixed space for 5 elements
int arr[] - size determined by initialization values
If int arr[5] = {1,2}, remaining elements initialized to 0
If int arr[] = {1,2,3}, array size becomes 3

questions.question: Write a C program to find the largest and smallest element in a one-dimensional array.
keyPoints: Initialize max and min with first element arr[0]
Traverse from index 1 to n-1
Compare each element with max and min
Update accordingly

questions.question: What is linear search? Write an algorithm and state its time complexity.
keyPoints: Start from first element, compare with target
Continue until element found or array ends
Time complexity: O(n) in worst case
Best case: O(1) if element at first position

questions.question: What is binary search and what is its prerequisite? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Binary search divides search space in half each step
Requires sorted array as prerequisite
Compare target with middle element, eliminate half
Time complexity: O(log n)

questions.question: Explain zero-based indexing in C arrays with an example.
keyPoints: In C, first element accessed using index 0
Valid indices range from 0 to size-1
For int arr[5], indices are 0,1,2,3,4
arr[0] is first element, arr[4] is last element

questions.question: Write a C program to sort n elements of a one-dimensional array using Selection Sort. Explain the algorithm and analyze its time complexity.
keyPoints: Algorithm: Find minimum element in unsorted portion, swap with first unsorted element
Repeat for remaining elements
Outer loop: i from 0 to n-2
Inner loop: j from i+1 to n-1 to find minimum
Time complexity: O(n²) - same for best, worst, average cases
Space complexity: O(1) - in-place sorting

questions.question: Write a C program to search an element in a one-dimensional array using Binary Search. Also explain why binary search cannot be directly applied to an unsorted array.
keyPoints: Binary search requires sorted array
Initialize low=0, high=n-1
While low <= high: mid = (low+high)/2
Compare arr[mid] with target, adjust low/high accordingly
Cannot use on unsorted because division into halves assumes ordered sequence
If unsorted, binary search gives incorrect results

questions.question: Write a C program to reverse a one-dimensional array in-place. Explain how the swapping works.
keyPoints: Use two-pointer technique
Initialize i=0 (start), j=n-1 (end)
Swap arr[i] and arr[j] using temporary variable
Increment i, decrement j until i >= j
Requires n/2 swaps, O(n) time

questions.question: Explain how arrays are passed to functions in C. Write a function to calculate the sum of array elements and explain the concept of array decay.
keyPoints: Array name without index passes base address
Function parameter: int arr[] or int *arr
Modifications affect original array (pass by reference)
Array decays to pointer - loses size information
Must pass size separately as n is not automatically known
